title: Under The New Hazcom Ghs Requirements
source_filename: Under The New Hazcom Ghs Requirements.docx

qas.q: OSHA's Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) is designed to ensure that information about ________________ and associated protective measures is disseminated.
Chemical manufacturers and importers must convey the hazard information they learn from their evaluations to downstream employers by means of labels on containers and:

Under the new HazCom/GHS requirements for Safety Data Sheets (SDS) they must now have 16 total sections.

(T/F)
Employers must provide employees with effective information and training on hazardous chemicals in their work area:
The noise exposure measurement your employers makes must include all continuous, intermittent, and _____________ noise within a range of 80 dB to 130 dB.
To determine whether employees need hearing protection, employers have to consider:
Audiometric testing monitors an employee's hearing:
Acute silicosis occurs after a few months or as long as 2 years following exposures to ______________ concentrations of respirable crystalline silica.
Symptoms of silicosis _______________, so workers should have a chest x-ray to see if there is lung damage.
qas.a: May or may not be obvious.

qas.q: Which of the following is an element of a hearing conservation program?
